message:
fix: stabilize flaky tests with serde_json preserve_order and deterministic ordering

- Enable serde_json `preserve_order` feature at workspace level to ensure
  consistent Map iteration order regardless of feature unification context
- Simplify tracking test to directly assert insertion order (count-desc,
  name-asc) now that IndexMap is guaranteed
- Fix deterministic ordering in timestamps, server path matching, and
  scrub operations to eliminate multi-agent compilation race flakiness
- Close beads br-gtg9, br-2wsr (flaky test fixes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2yw0","title":"Guard parity: unit + integration tests","description":"## Objective
Add unit/integration tests for guard behavior beyond the E2E repo test.

## Scope
- Path matching (pathspec + fnmatch fallback).
- Pre‑commit scanning (staged files, rename handling).
- Pre‑push scanning (commit range).
- Gate on `WORKTREES_ENABLED`/`GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED` per legacy guard.py.

## Tests
- Unit tests for pathspec matches and denylist/allowlist behavior.
- Integration tests using temp git repo with staged changes and renames.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store guard decision logs under `tests/artifacts/guard/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Guard behavior matches legacy for staged/renamed files and gate flags.
2. Unit/integration tests are deterministic and isolate temp repos.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralDog","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:20:57.657586279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T12:36:36.933732035Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T12:36:36.933706597Z","close_reason":"Added guard unit/integration tests for gate parsing + pre-push path enumeration (rev-list + diff-tree), including renames + net-diff-empty case; refactored truthy parsing for testability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2yw0","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:21:03.377702360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-33ha.7.3","title":"E2E tests for tool metrics and query tracking endpoints (resource://tooling/*)","description":"Create tests/e2e/test_tool_metrics.sh that exercises the tool metrics and query tracking observability endpoints.

## Test Cases (10+ assertions)

### Case 1: Initial metrics state
- Start fresh server, no tool calls yet
- Read resource://tooling/metrics
- Verify response contains "tools" array with 23+ tool entries
- Verify all entries have calls=0, errors=0 initially
- Verify health_level field is present

### Case 2: Metrics increment after tool calls
- Call ensure_project → register_agent → send_message
- Read resource://tooling/metrics again
- Verify ensure_project calls >= 1
- Verify register_agent calls >= 1
- Verify send_message calls >= 1
- Verify other tools still have calls=0

### Case 3: Error counting
- Call a tool with invalid params (e.g., send_message with empty to=[])
- Read metrics → verify that tool's errors count incremented
- Verify error count <= calls count for all tools

### Case 4: Tool cluster and capability metadata
- Read resource://tooling/metrics
- Verify each tool has a non-empty cluster field
- Verify each tool has a capabilities array
- Verify ensure_project is in "infrastructure" cluster

### Case 5: Query tracking via resource
- Read resource://tooling/directory
- Verify it contains all 7 clusters
- Verify playbooks and output_formats sections exist

### Case 6: Metrics with tool filtering
- Start server with AM_TOOL_FILTER=core
- Read resource://tooling/metrics
- Verify only core-profile tools are listed (fewer than full set)
- Verify filtered-out tools are not in metrics response

## Logging & Quality
- Source e2e_lib.sh, e2e_case_banner per case
- Use stdio transport for tool calls, capture all responses to artifact dir
- Parse JSON responses with jq for field extraction
- e2e_assert_eq for exact counts, e2e_assert_contains for string fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- All assertions pass
- Metrics are live (not static), reflecting actual tool call history
- No test pollution between cases (fresh server per test or sequential counting)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:49:35.179584305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:13.304803194Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:13.304780261Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-33ha.7.3","depends_on_id":"br-33ha.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:51:04.652068191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-33ha.7.3","depends_on_id":"br-33ha.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:49:35.179584305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-36w","title":"Config parity: .env loading + env var coverage","description":"## Objective
Match legacy configuration behavior, including `.env` loading and full env var coverage.

## Scope
- `.env` loading behavior (python‑decouple parity):
  - if .env missing → empty repository fallback; env vars only.
  - canonical env var set only if missing when alias present (LLM bridge).
- Ensure all env vars from spec are supported:
  - WORKTREES_ENABLED + GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED gate
  - ALLOW_ABSOLUTE_ATTACHMENT_PATHS
  - TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED, LOG_RICH_ENABLED
  - NOTIFICATIONS_* defaults
  - HTTP_* (CORS/JWT/RBAC/rate limit)
  - LLM_* defaults
- CSV parsing for list settings (roles, tools, clusters).
- Match default values exactly.

## Tests
- Unit tests for .env presence/absence handling.
- Table‑driven env parsing tests for bools/ints/CSV lists.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store parsed config snapshots under `tests/artifacts/config/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Rust config matches legacy defaults and parsing semantics.
2. `.env` loading behavior matches python‑decouple (graceful missing file).
3. All env variables from spec are supported and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:09.094825172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832107234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832088128Z","close_reason":"Implemented .env loading + env var coverage + tests; checks blocked by sqlmodel-sqlite duplicate defs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-36w","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:12.895192349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3gs","title":"Share: sign manifest + encrypt bundle + deterministic ZIP","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 13–15: Ed25519 signing, age encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope
- **sign_manifest**: sign manifest.json with Ed25519 seed; optionally output public key.
- **encrypt_bundle**: age encryption when recipients provided; output `.age` file; retain clear bundle if required by legacy.
- **package_directory_as_zip**: deterministic ZIP (stable ordering, timestamps, compression settings).

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and age encrypt/decrypt.
- Integration tests for deterministic ZIP hash.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store signatures, public keys, and zip hashes under `tests/artifacts/share/crypto/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Manifest signatures validate correctly with provided public key.
2. Encryption outputs readable bundles via `share decrypt`.
3. ZIP packaging is deterministic (stable hash across runs).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:36.383969609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811535263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811510687Z","close_reason":"Verified sign/encrypt/zip implementation via mcp-agent-mail-share tests (age roundtrip + deterministic zip)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3gs","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:41.980704900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"br-3gs","author":"CopperOwl","text":"Claimed br-3gs.

Plan:
- Audit existing share crypto + deterministic ZIP implementation in crates/mcp-agent-mail-share for legacy parity gaps.
- Add/adjust tests (signature verify, age encrypt/decrypt, deterministic ZIP hash) and required tests/artifacts/share/crypto/<timestamp>/ artifacts.
- Run gates (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-share) and close br-3gs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3h94","title":"Error type mapping parity (CAPABILITY_DENIED, NOT_FOUND, etc.)","description":"## Objective
Ensure error type mapping and JSON error shapes match legacy across tools/resources.

- Map internal errors to these codes consistently.
- Ensure tool/resource responses include legacy error fields and HTTP error status where applicable.

## Tests

- Conformance tests for representative error cases (missing fields, invalid args, capability denied, not found).

## Logging/Artifacts

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All error codes and shapes match legacy.
2. Conformance tests include error cases for each category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:30.739623609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:17:59.988606898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:17:59.988584837Z","close_reason":"completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h94","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:35.625592551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. Signature validation and age decrypt succeed on valid inputs and fail loudly on invalid.
3. Mutual‑exclusion flag handling matches legacy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:17.420980860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917640762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917621787Z","close_reason":"Added CLI integration tests for share verify/decrypt parity; validated exit codes + defaults","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.284948962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:23.973796081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. Tests are deterministic and isolated to temp DB paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:05.665286231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363640110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363620834Z","close_reason":"Implemented backup snapshot + scope parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ave","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:11.619634150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ekhi","title":"Bug: CLI acks/list-acks queries referenced nonexistent inbox table","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"DustyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:50.759265420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:50.759265420Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:50.759265420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-gtg9","title":"Fix flaky test: to_dict_per_table_sorted_by_count_desc_then_name_asc","description":"Test in tracking.rs fails intermittently in full suite but passes individually. Root cause: serde_json feature unification timing - the order_probe check for preserve_order may give inconsistent results depending on compilation order. The probe detects BTreeMap backend but actual per_table map preserves insertion order. Consider removing the probe and always asserting insertion-order (since indexmap feature IS unified in the workspace).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:32:36.906218063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:36:17.398892938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:36:17.398862601Z","close_reason":"Fixed: added preserve_order feature to workspace serde_json dep, simplified test to always assert insertion order","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/am serve works without manual exports in a typical dev setup (token file present).
- With default config (HTTP_PATH unset), both POST /api/ and POST /mcp/ reach the MCP handler.
- Full quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.
